# Draftlab Scaffold

> A modern website starter built with Astro v6, Tailwind CSS v4, and React v19.
> Provides a flexible page-building system, type-safe content collections, and
> an integrated Git-based CMS (PagesCMS). Pre-configured for Netlify deployment.

Scaffold is a template, not a finished site â€” use it as the starting point
for your own content-focused website. Once installed, everything is editable
either by hand (YAML/JSON/Markdown) or through a visual CMS interface. The
articles, people, partners, and resources that ship with the live demo are
placeholder content meant to be replaced.

## Quick start

Requires Node.js v22.12+ and Git.

    npx create-astro --template draftlab-org/scaffold
    cd your-project-name
    npm install
    npm run dev

Dev server runs at http://localhost:4321. Build with `npm run build`
(static output in `dist/`); preview with `npm run preview`.

## Deployment

- Pre-configured for **Netlify** via `@astrojs/netlify`. Connect the repo,
  push to `main`, done â€” no extra config.
- Works with any static host (Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, GitHub Pages) by
  swapping the adapter in `astro.config.mjs`.

## Updating from upstream

Scaffold is designed to be forked. Downstream sites can pull future Scaffold
updates without losing content, assets, styles, or branding:

    git remote add template https://github.com/draftlab-org/scaffold.git
    npm run update-from-scaffold

`scripts/scaffold-update.sh` (wrapped as `npm run update-from-scaffold`):

- Registers the `ours` merge driver in local git config (`merge=ours` in
  `.gitattributes` is not a built-in driver; without this Git silently falls
  back to 3-way merge and conflicts every protected file)
- Fetches `template/main` and merges. First merge after `npx create-astro
  --template ...` has no shared root, so the script adds
  `--allow-unrelated-histories -X theirs` â€” without `-X theirs`, every
  diverged file becomes an add/add conflict; with it, unprotected files take
  upstream while protected paths still keep downstream via `merge=ours`
- Resolves modify/delete conflicts in protected paths by re-applying the
  downstream deletion (the `merge=ours` driver only handles content conflicts)
- Auto-removes brand-new upstream files in `src/content/`, `src/assets/`, and
  `public/` to keep demo content out of production (`src/styles/` is excluded
  since new stylesheets may be required by new components)
- If a new directory appears under `src/content/` (indicating a new content
  collection), prints an alert at end of run pointing to https://scaffold.org

A checked-in `.gitattributes` marks these paths as `merge=ours` (downstream
always wins on conflict):

- `src/content/**` â€” content collections
- `src/assets/**` â€” uploaded images, logos, artwork
- `src/styles/**` â€” theme tokens, typography, component utilities
- `public/**` â€” favicons, OG images, robots.txt, branding
- `fonts.config.mjs` â€” font choices (see Architecture)

Everything else (components, layouts, utils, `astro.config.mjs`, `package.json`)
merges normally and consumers receive template improvements.

## Documentation

- [How to use Scaffold](https://scaffold.draftlab.org/how-to-use): Full guide covering installation, page building, content collections, PagesCMS, and deployment
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/draftlab-org/scaffold): Source code, issues, releases
- [README](https://github.com/draftlab-org/scaffold/blob/main/README.md): Install instructions, commands, project structure

## Content model

Content lives in `src/content/` with schemas in `src/content.config.ts`
(Astro's Content Layer API). Nine collections ship by default:

- **Site** (`src/content/site/config.json`): Singleton with title, description, SEO defaults, social links, cookie consent, archived banners
- **Pages** (`src/content/pages/*.yaml`): Each file is a route, built from a `sections` array
- **Navigation** (`src/content/navigation/*.json`): Header and footer menus
- **Articles** (`src/content/articles/*.md`): Markdown blog posts with frontmatter
- **Docs** (`src/content/docs/*.md`): Multi-chapter markdown documentation, grouped by `chapter`/`chapterOrder` and sorted by `order`
- **People** (`src/content/people/*.json`): Team profiles
- **Partners** (`src/content/partners/*.json`): Organizations with logo, URL, category
- **Resources** (`src/content/resources/*.json`): Publications, reports, guides
- **Categories** (`src/content/categories/*.json`): Category definitions used by filters

All collections use a unified `status` field (`draft`, `published`, `archived`).
Drafts are only visible in `npm run dev` or with `PUBLIC_PREVIEW=true`.

## Page sections

Every page is assembled from sections. Available types:

- `hero` â€” Full-width banner with title, subtitle, optional background image
- `richText` â€” Markdown with optional table of contents
- `button` â€” Row of call-to-action buttons
- `card` â€” Grid of cards with markdown, images, links
- `people` â€” Grid of team profiles, filterable by category
- `partners` â€” Grid of partner logos, filterable by category
- `featuredPartners` â€” Curated partner row
- `articlesRoll` â€” Feed of recent articles
- `resourcesRoll` â€” Feed of recent resources
- `flexi` â€” Nested container for combining sections

A GitHub Action (`.github/workflows/auto-fix-permalinks.yml`) keeps page
filenames and `permalink` fields in sync automatically.

## Embedded media in markdown

Any rich-text or markdown body (article and doc bodies, `richText` page
sections, and PagesCMS rich-text fields) supports embeds via a plain markdown
link whose text is the literal string `EmbedLink`:

    [EmbedLink](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=...)
    [EmbedLink](https://vimeo.com/...)
    [EmbedLink](https://bsky.app/profile/<handle>/post/<id>)
    [EmbedLink](https://<instance>/@<user>/<status-id>)

The link must be the sole content of its paragraph. Implemented as a single
remark plugin (`src/lib/remark-embed-link.ts`) wired into both markdown
pipelines: Astro's built-in markdown (via `markdown.remarkPlugins` in
`astro.config.mjs`) and the custom `src/utils/renderMarkdown.ts` processor
chain. No MDX migration is required.

- **YouTube / Vimeo**: URLâ†’ID transform, rendered as `<lite-youtube>` /
  `<lite-vimeo>` web components (lazy poster + click-to-load). The
  `lite-youtube-embed` / `lite-vimeo-embed` modules are registered once in
  `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`.
- **Bluesky**: fetched at build time from `public.api.bsky.app` (handle
  resolution + `app.bsky.feed.getPosts`); rendered as a static post card.
- **Mastodon**: fetched at build time from the source instance's
  `/api/v1/statuses/:id` endpoint; rendered as a static post card.
- **Fallback**: any URL that doesn't match a provider, and any failed
  build-time fetch (deleted post, rate limit, network error), leaves the
  original link in place and emits a build warning.

Build-time tradeoff: each Bluesky/Mastodon embed is one outbound HTTP request
during `astro build`. Post edits or deletions only reflect on the next deploy.

To add a provider, extend `buildEmbedHTML()` in `src/lib/remark-embed-link.ts`.

## PagesCMS

Scaffold ships with a complete `.pages.yml` configuration for
[PagesCMS](https://pagescms.org) â€” a free, open-source, Git-based CMS.

- Sign in at [app.pagescms.org](https://app.pagescms.org) with GitHub
- Select your repository; PagesCMS reads `.pages.yml` and configures the editor automatically
- No API keys, no database, no separate hosting â€” edits commit directly to your repo and trigger a rebuild

## Architecture

- **Stack**: Astro v6, React v19, Tailwind CSS v4 (Vite plugin), MDX
- **Components**: Atomic design â€” `atoms` â†’ `molecules` â†’ `organisms` â†’ `sections`
- **TypeScript**: Strict mode with path aliases (`@components/*`, `@utils/*`, `@lib/*`, `@content/*`, `@styles/*`, `@layouts/*`, `@assets/*`, `@pages/*`)
- **Icons**: [unplugin-icons](https://github.com/unplugin/unplugin-icons) + [@iconify/json](https://icon-sets.iconify.design/) â€” import as `~icons/[collection]/[icon-name]`
- **Styling**: Tailwind v4 with `@theme` tokens in `src/styles/colors.css`, `typography.css`, `breakpoints.css`
- **Fonts**: Declared in `fonts.config.mjs` at repo root (split out from `astro.config.mjs` so consumers can swap fonts without conflicting with template updates). Three slots â€” `--font-sans`, `--font-serif`, `--font-mono` â€” loaded from [Bunny Fonts](https://fonts.bunny.net) by default. Change a font by editing `name` and `weights` in `fonts.config.mjs`; the CSS variable names should not be renamed.

## API endpoints

All content collections are automatically exposed as JSON at build time via `src/pages/api/[collection].json.ts`:

- `/api/pages.json`
- `/api/articles.json`
- `/api/docs.json`
- `/api/people.json`
- `/api/partners.json`
- `/api/resources.json`
- `/api/navigation.json`
- `/api/categories.json`
- `/api/site.json`

## External references

- [Astro documentation](https://docs.astro.build)
- [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com)
- [PagesCMS documentation](https://pagescms.org/docs)
- [Atomic design principles](https://atomicdesign.bradfrost.com/chapter-2/)
